title: Twenty-two years
summary: Two boyhood friends meet in Madrid for the first time in twenty-two years. They catch up — losses, jobs, an old anger — and then they go for coffee.

body.speaker: Carlos
body.text: I would not have recognised you.

body.speaker: Tomas
body.text: I have a beard now. You did not have grey hair.

body.speaker: Tomas
body.text: Twenty-two. You went to Mexico in 2003.

body.speaker: Carlos
body.text: Yes. I came back six months ago.

body.speaker: Tomas
body.text: Your sister. She still works at the bakery on Calle del Sol.

body.speaker: Carlos
body.text: Of course. The bakery is the newspaper of the family.

body.speaker: Carlos
body.text: It was four years ago. I am here now. I came back to Spain because she asked me to. Before, at the end.

body.speaker: Tomas
body.text: I am still here. I still teach at the school. I am the principal now.

body.speaker: Tomas
body.text: The same. Mrs Garcia retired. She left me her job. She was eighty-four.

body.speaker: Tomas
body.text: Carlos, we are old men now.

body.speaker: Tomas
body.text: I will tell you the truth. When you left, I was very angry with you. For ten years. Then I was sad. Then I was nothing.

body.speaker: Tomas
body.text: You should have. But you did not. And here we are.

grammarNotes.title: Past simple with time expressions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Quá khứ đơn dùng để kể những sự việc đã hoàn tất, đi kèm mốc thời gian rõ ràng. Trong bài: 'You went to Mexico in 2003.' (with 'in + năm') 'I came back six months ago.' ('ago' sau khoảng thời gian) 'She left me her job.' (sự việc hoàn tất) 'When you left, I was very angry.' ('when' nối hai mệnh đề quá khứ). Câu 'I should have written' giới thiệu 'should have + V3' — sự tiếc nuối về quá khứ.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The past simple is for finished actions, often with a time expression. 'You went to Mexico in 2003.' ('in + year') 'I came back six months ago.' ('ago' follows a length of time) 'She left me her job.' (a completed action). 'When you left, I was very angry.' joins two past clauses. Note 'I should have written' — 'should have + past participle' shows regret about the past.

translationVi:
Carlos: Tomas.
Tomas: Carlos.
Carlos: Tôi không nhận ra cậu nữa.
Tomas: Giờ tớ có râu. Hồi đó cậu chưa có tóc bạc.
Carlos: Hai mươi năm rồi.
Tomas: Hai hai năm. Cậu sang Mexico năm 2003.
Carlos: Đúng. Tớ về lại sáu tháng trước.
Tomas: Tớ có nghe.
Carlos: Từ ai?
Tomas: Chị cậu. Chị vẫn làm ở tiệm bánh phố Calle del Sol.
Carlos: Đương nhiên. Tiệm bánh là tờ báo của cả nhà.
Tomas: Vợ cậu sao rồi?
Carlos: Vợ tớ mất năm 2019. Ung thư.
Tomas: Carlos. Tớ xin lỗi.
Carlos: Bốn năm rồi. Giờ tớ ở đây. Tớ về Tây Ban Nha vì cô ấy dặn — trước lúc đi.
Tomas: Lý do tốt.
Carlos: Còn cậu?
Tomas: Tớ vẫn ở đây. Tớ vẫn dạy trường cũ. Giờ là hiệu trưởng.
Carlos: Trường mình ngày xưa á?
Tomas: Cùng trường. Cô Garcia về hưu. Cô để lại chỗ cho tớ. Cô tám mươi tư tuổi rồi.
Carlos: Cô Garcia. Tớ nhớ chứ.
Tomas: Carlos, hai đứa mình thành ông già rồi.
Carlos: Đúng vậy.
Tomas: Tớ nói thật. Khi cậu đi, tớ giận cậu lắm. Mười năm. Sau đó tớ buồn. Sau đó tớ không gì cả.
Carlos: Tớ đáng lẽ phải viết thư.
Tomas: Đáng lẽ thế. Nhưng cậu không viết. Và giờ ngồi đây.
Carlos: Giờ ngồi đây.
Tomas: Cà phê chứ?
Carlos: Cà phê.

questions.prompt: How long has Carlos been back in Spain?
questions.hint: Carlos says it just after the year.

questions.prompt: How did Tomas hear about Carlos's return?
options: From Carlos's sister, who works at the bakery on Calle del Sol
He saw Carlos on the street.
questions.explanation: 'Your sister. She still works at the bakery on Calle del Sol.'
questions.hint: Tomas names who he heard from.

questions.prompt: What does Carlos mean by 'The bakery is the newspaper of the family'?
options: His family's news spreads through the bakery — that is where everyone hears things.
questions.explanation: It is the place where family news travels — informally, through the daily customers.
questions.hint: Think about what a newspaper does.
Carlos answers Tomas's question about his wife.

questions.prompt: Why did Carlos come back to Spain?
options: His wife asked him to, before she died.
questions.explanation: 'I came back to Spain because she asked me to. Before, at the end.'

options: The principal of their old school
questions.explanation: 'I am the principal now.' Of 'our old school.'

questions.prompt: How does Tomas describe his feelings after Carlos left?
options: Angry for ten years, then sad, then nothing.
Only sad for a short time.
questions.explanation: 'When you left, I was very angry with you. For ten years. Then I was sad. Then I was nothing.'
questions.hint: A sequence of feelings, in order.

questions.prompt: What does Carlos say he should have done?
questions.explanation: 'I should have written.' 'You should have. But you did not.'

options: With a quiet acknowledgement and an offer of coffee.
questions.hint: Look at the last two lines.

cloze.template:
Carlos: Tomas.
Tomas: Carlos.
Carlos: I would not have {{b1}} you.
Tomas: I have a beard now. You did not have grey hair.
Carlos: Twenty years.
Tomas: Twenty-two. You went to {{b2}} in 2003.
Carlos: Yes. I came back six {{b3}} ago.
Tomas: I heard.
Carlos: From who?
Tomas: Your sister. She still works at the {{b4}} on Calle del Sol.
Carlos: Of course. The bakery is the newspaper of the family.
Tomas: How is your wife?
Carlos: My wife died in {{b5}}. Cancer.
Tomas: Carlos. I am sorry.
Carlos: It was four years ago. I am here now. I came back to Spain because she asked me to. Before, at the end.
Tomas: That is a good {{b6}}.
Carlos: And you?
Tomas: I am still here. I still teach at the school. I am the {{b7}} now.
Carlos: Of our old school?
Tomas: The same. Mrs Garcia retired. She left me her job. She was eighty-four.
Carlos: Mrs Garcia. I remember her.
Tomas: Carlos, we are {{b8}} men now.
Carlos: We are.
Tomas: I will tell you the truth. When you left, I was very angry with you. For ten years. Then I was {{b9}}. Then I was nothing.
Carlos: I should have written.
Tomas: You should have. But you did not. And here we are.
Carlos: Here we are.
Tomas: Coffee?
Carlos: {{b10}}.

blanks.explanation: He would not have recognised him.
His sister works at the bakery.
Tomas is the principal of the school now.
After the anger came the sadness.
The dialogue ends with the simple acceptance 'Coffee.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: Tomas says he was angry, then sad, then 'nothing.' Have you ever lost touch with a close friend and noticed your feelings move through stages like that? What did 'nothing' feel like — and what would it take to move past it?
